sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Bologna have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, with a tendency to concede first and struggle with clean sheets (13 games without one). Fiorentina, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 3 matches, with 1 win and 2 draws, showing resilience with late goals and better attacking output recently, as seen in their 5-1 thrashing of Udinese.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between Bologna and Fiorentina have been tight, with Fiorentina winning 3-2 in May 2025 and drawing 2-2 in October 2025. Both games saw goals from both sides, indicating a high likelihood of an open, competitive match with defensive vulnerabilities on display.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Bologna, sitting 8th with 30 points, have scored 29 goals (5th in the league) but underperformed their xG by -3.46, showing inefficiency in attack despite creating big chances. Fiorentina, struggling in 18th with just 14 points, have scored 21 goals but conceded 31, with their recent form suggesting a slight uptick in confidence.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Bologna, Riccardo Orsolini (7 goals, avg. rating 6.92) and Santiago Castro (6 goals, avg. rating 6.93) are critical in attack, with Orsolini also standing out in past head-to-heads. Fiorentina’s Rolando Mandragora (5 goals, avg. rating 7.0) and Nicolò Fagioli (3 assists, avg. rating 7.12) could be decisive with their creativity and finishing.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Bologna’s home advantage at Renato Dall’Ara and Sofascore’s 55% probability for a home win, they are favorites, especially with Fiorentina’s poor away form and league position. However, Fiorentina’s recent momentum and ability to score late could make this a close contest, likely with both teams finding the net.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Fiorentina lead Bologna 2-0 at the Renato Dall'Ara. Fiorentina have been clinical in front of goal, with Rolando Mandragora and Roberto Piccoli finding the net in the 19th and 45th minutes respectively. Bologna, despite dominating possession, have struggled to create meaningful chances and have failed to register a shot on target. Fiorentina's efficiency in attack and ability to capitalize on their opportunities have given them a comfortable cushion going into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Bologna's struggles in attack
halftimeSections.text: Bologna have controlled much of the ball in the first half, but their attacking output has been underwhelming. They’ve entered the final third frequently and earned several corners, yet they’ve failed to convert these into dangerous opportunities. Their expected goals reflect a lack of cutting edge in the final third. Players like Jens Odgaard and Riccardo Orsolini have been unable to make a significant impact, and the team will need a tactical shift or increased intensity to break through Fiorentina’s defense in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Fiorentina's clinical edge
halftimeSections.text: Fiorentina have been far more effective in their attacking play, making the most of fewer entries into the final third. Their expected goals are significantly higher than Bologna’s, showcasing their ability to create high-quality chances. Roberto Piccoli and Rolando Mandragora have been pivotal, with Piccoli’s late first-half goal doubling their lead. Albert Guðmundsson has also played a key role with an assist, demonstrating Fiorentina’s threat on the counter and in transition moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Bologna start with some early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, Fiorentina quickly shifted the momentum with a strong attacking spell that led to their opening goal. While Bologna had periods of sustained pressure midway through the half, they couldn’t capitalize. Fiorentina, on the other hand, grew in confidence with bursts of attacking intent, especially towards the end of the half, culminating in their second goal. These momentum shifts highlight Fiorentina’s ability to strike at key moments, while Bologna’s efforts lacked the final punch.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Defensive and midfield dynamics
halftimeSections.text: Bologna have shown some resilience in midfield, winning a decent percentage of duels and aerial battles. However, their defensive organization has been exposed by Fiorentina’s sharp attacking moves. Fiorentina’s defense, while not heavily tested, has held firm, with players like Pietro Comuzzo and Marin Pongračić making crucial clearances. In midfield, Rolando Mandragora has been a standout for Fiorentina, not only scoring but also contributing with key passes. Bologna will need players like Remo Freuler to step up if they are to regain control in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Bologna are likely to come out with renewed urgency in the second half, given the two-goal deficit. They may push for an early goal to shift the game’s momentum, possibly introducing fresh legs to inject creativity in attack. Fiorentina, on the other hand, might adopt a more conservative approach to protect their lead, relying on counter-attacks to exploit spaces left by Bologna’s forward push. Given Fiorentina’s clinical finishing so far, they remain the favorites to add to their tally, especially if Bologna overcommit in search of a comeback.
